data.title: Walsh's Investment Empire
data.description:
Tao Zhu, Jinse Finance

Summary

April 14, 2026, according to Reuters: Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Kevin Warsh listed dozens of future-oriented assets in his latest financial disclosure filing, including Elon Musk's SpaceX and the prediction platform Polymarket. The filing shows that Warsh has made dozens of seemingly small investments in a range of emerging and almost science fiction-like projects.

Warsh's major holdings bring his assets to over $100 million, including two stakes in Juggernaut Fund LP worth over $50 million.

Among his dozens of other holdings, Warsh's traditional central banker image has shifted. These holdings are listed under a company called DCM Investments 10 LLC, with a market value of less than $500,000. In these holdings, Warsh's image gradually transforms into a vision of the future: digital AI avatars offering advice, AI-driven art, new herpes vaccines, long-acting reversible male contraceptives, and decentralized derivatives trading.

This article reviews Warsh's investment empire. I. Warsh's Investment Strategy According to the U.S. Government Office of Ethics (OGE) Public Financial Disclosure Report (Form 278e), Warsh's personally controlled entities include: Vicarage LLC (a personal consulting firm providing macroeconomic/investment advice to large institutions); Vicarage Corporation (revenue collection, investment management, tax/structural optimization); and multiple LLC entities (asset holding vehicles). The companies he has consulted include: Duquesne Family Office (founded by George Soros and Stanley Druckenmiller, a family office specializing in macro investments including interest rates, exchange rates, and stocks, and one of the world's top macro investors); Cerberus Capital (a private equity fund specializing in mergers and acquisitions, restructuring, and investments in sectors such as banking, defense, and real estate, and one of Wall Street's largest private equity firms); GoldenTree (a credit hedge fund investing in bonds, specializing in profiting from crisis assets and junk bonds); and Heitman (a real estate investment management company specializing in commercial real estate, REITs, and real estate funds, and a global institutional investor in real estate). Companies where he serves on the board: UPS (one of the world's largest express delivery companies, a representative of the real economy); Coupang (a South Korean e-commerce company with its own logistics system, a high-growth technology company, and a representative of Asian technology stocks); Bessemer Securities (backed by the long-established American wealth management family Bessemer Trust, specializing in investment management and family wealth, and one of the oldest financial family systems in the United States). Other: He is also a Shepard Family Visiting Fellow at the Hoover Institution at Stanford University. II. Warsh's Income 1. Consulting Income Duquesne Family Office: $10.2 million; GoldenTree: $1.55 million; Cerberus: $750,000; Heitman: $650,000. These incomes from top hedge funds constitute his primary source of income. 2. Speaker Revenue (Annual) Warburg Pincus: $90,000 (A top global private equity fund, primarily investing in unlisted companies in the technology, finance, and healthcare sectors; one of the oldest and most influential PE firms globally); State Street: $135,000 (An asset management and custodian bank, helping pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and other institutions safeguard assets and manage ETFs; one of the world's three largest ETF giants and a global financial infrastructure company); Eli Lilly: $122,500 (A large US pharmaceutical company, researching and selling drugs such as diabetes medications and weight-loss drugs; one of the world's highest-valued pharmaceutical companies); Brevan Howard: $750,000 (Europe's strongest global macro hedge fund, covering interest rates, exchange rates, inflation, and macroeconomics). Warsh's annual speaking revenue has exceeded one million US dollars. 3. Academic Income Stanford: $170,000. III. Warsh's Core Asset Structure 1. Cash and Funds Bank Accounts: Millions of US Dollars; Money Market Funds: JPM Treasury MMF: $1-5 million (JPM Treasury MMF: Money Market Fund, primarily investing in short-term US Treasury bonds and government bonds). 2. Index Funds Vanguard 500 Index: $250,000-500,000 (S&P 500 Index Fund, primarily investing in the 500 largest US companies); Vanguard Balanced Fund: $1-5 million (A mixed fund of stocks and bonds).

3. Stock Holdings

Coupang:

$1 million - $5 million

RSU (Unlocked)

UPS:

Virtual Stocks: $1 million - $5 million

RSU: $1 million - $5 million

Dividends: Up to $1 million

4. Private Equity Funds

Juggernaut Fund LP (Private Investment Fund)

Value: Over $50 million (single investment)

Income: Over $5 million

IV. Which Sectors is Warsh Bullish On?

1. AI

  • Recraft (a designer tool that generates vector graphics using AI)

  • Volt (AI security and surveillance)

  • 11x (using AI to replace sales/customer service positions)

  • Delphi AI (creating AI avatars (imitating speech, knowledge, and style))

  • Hebbia (AI financial analysis)

  • Krea (AI image generation)

  • Lindy (AI assistant, automating tasks)

  • Sesame AI (voice AI, including dialogue and customer service)

  • Showrunner (AI-generated movies and storylines)

2. Cryptocurrency

  • Polymarket (Prediction Market)

  • Solana (Blockchain)

  • Optimism (Ethereum Extension)

  • dYdX (Decentralized Exchange)

  • Compound (Crypto Lending)

  • Polychain (Crypto Fund)

  • Lemon Cash (Crypto Finance)

  • Tenderly (Ethereum Development)

3. Aerospace

  • SpaceX (Aerospace + AI)

4. Biotechnology

  • Outpace Bio (Protein Engineering, Cancer Treatment)

  • Locus Biosciences (Gene Editing Antibacterial) Rational Vaccines (Vaccine Development) Unnatural Products (Drug Discovery) 5. Fintech Stashfin (Emerging Market Digital Bank) Alpaca (Financial API) Moov (Payment Infrastructure) Nomad (Cross-border Banking Services) Method Financial (User Debt Data Interface) 6. Consumer/Platform list-paddingleft-2">

  • Partiful (Event Social)

  • Snackpass (Dining Social)

  • Wanderlog (Travel Planning)

  • Wonderschool (Childcare Services)

7. Robotics / Automation

  • Cafe X (Robot Coffee Making)

  • OSARO (Warehouse Robot)

  • Watney Robotics (Data Center Robot)

8. "Science Fiction" Projects

  • Cionic (Bionic Enhancement Suit, Exoskeleton/Assisted Motion)

  • Friend ( AI Companion Devices) Persona AI (Persistent AI Personality) Zero Gravity (AI Blockchain) V. Other Assets 1. Spouse Assets Estée Lauder: Stocks: Greater than $1 million Dividends: $50,000 to $100,000 Retirement Fund: Up to $1 million (Wash's wife, Jane Lauder, is the daughter of Ronald Lauder, a member of the founding family of Estée Lauder.) Eventbrite (Event Ticketing Platform): Stocks: $500,000 to $1 million $10,000 Other: Restaurant investment Casa Cruz (high-end private restaurant, members-only club, targeting wealthy social circles, serving as Warsh's social resource entry point) Horse farms (two) Club loans Treasury bonds (I-Bonds) In summary, Warsh's asset structure is characterized by a focus on traditional finance as the core, supplemented by investments in cutting-edge technology, a portfolio extremely rare among central bank candidates. VI. The Impact of Warsh's Appointment Warsh is known for his pro-market views, and his appointment may lead US monetary policy in a new direction. Many believe that the upcoming Senate confirmation hearing is a crucial moment in determining the future direction of the Federal Reserve. Policy Direction
    The Federal Reserve's policy rate remains within the 3.5% to 3.75% range. Recent Fed meeting minutes and official statements indicate that inflation, oil prices, and geopolitical conflicts are further delaying expectations of rate cuts, with some officials even reopening the possibility of future rate hikes.

    Wash enjoys a clear hawkish reputation in the market and is widely considered to place more emphasis on inflation control than Powell and to be more cautious about balance sheet expansion.

    Reuters previously pointed out that investors believed he might support a simpler banking regulatory framework and maintain a stricter stance on the Fed's balance sheet; therefore, the market once thought he was the kind of chairman who "might cut rates, but wouldn't easily loosen policy."

    The problem is that the current macroeconomic environment does not support an "easy shift." Fed meeting minutes show that officials have already discussed the oil price shock triggered by the Middle East war and the potential risk of a rebound in inflation.

    In response, Wall Street firms have postponed their expectations for interest rate cuts in 2026, with some even ceasing to anticipate any rate cuts this year. This means that if Warsh officially takes office in May, he will not be facing a market waiting for easing policies, but rather a market already readjusted by oil prices and inflation. Financial Market Direction If Warsh is confirmed to take office in May, the initial market reaction will typically not come from the real economy, but from asset pricing. Recently, investors have viewed Warsh as a "more disciplined" candidate for chairman; once this expectation solidifies, the dollar tends to strengthen, and long-term Treasury yields may rise first, as the market prices in expectations of fewer rate cuts and tighter financial conditions. Meanwhile, gold and growth stocks may face pressure in the opposite direction. Gold is sensitive to both real interest rates and the dollar; if the Fed prioritizes curbing inflation rather than supporting economic growth, gold's upside potential will be limited. Growth stocks, especially highly valued tech stocks, are more susceptible to changes in the discount rate. Warsh's appointment marked a macroeconomic turning point; the discount rates for the dollar, bonds, gold, tech stocks, and financial stocks all needed to be recalculated. The real challenge wasn't adapting to the person, but rather the Fed's more cautious approach and lower tolerance for error.

data.title: Steve Blank: Startups that have been in the AI ​​era for more than two years should consider starting over.
data.description:
Introduction:

The author of this article, Steve Blank, is very famous in the Silicon Valley startup circle, known as the "Father of the Lean Startup." He wrote *The Four Steps to the Epiphany* and is the originator of the Customer Development methodology.

Eric Ries' *The Lean Startup* is developed based on his theories. He has taught entrepreneurship courses at Stanford, Berkeley, and Columbia Universities, and the National Science Foundation's I-Corps project is also based on his methodology.

Steve Blank recently had coffee with a founder he had invested in and discovered that the person had been working diligently for six years, completely unaware that the outside world had changed.

He wrote this article based on that, and his core point is straightforward: If your company is more than two years old, your business plan is likely outdated. AI is reshaping development speed, team size, pricing models, and competitive barriers. Founders still using a 2024 script are unlikely to secure their next round of funding. For readers who are starting a business or following the tech and venture capital scene, this firsthand observation from across the ocean is worth reading.

The following is a full translation：

If your company is more than two years old, many of your initial assumptions may no longer hold true.

You need to stop what you're doing—whether it's writing code, developing products, hiring, or raising funds—and first look at what's happening around you. Otherwise, your company will die.

Anxiety Triggered by a Cup of Coffee I just had a cup of coffee with Chris. Chris is a founder I invested in six years ago, and since then he's been working tirelessly on: 1) a complex autonomous system problem, 2) in an existing market, and 3) with a unique business model. Chris is now preparing to launch his first large-scale funding round. Looking at his investor deck, I noticed a problem: while he's been working so hard these past few years, the outside world has changed dramatically. The autonomous systems software barrier he spent five years building is becoming increasingly unremarkable. Ukraine's autonomous drones and ground vehicles have spawned dozens, even hundreds, of companies with larger teams and more funding, all doing the same thing. Chris has been vying for adoption in his niche market (a market that certainly needs disruption, but the old players are still holding on), while a demand for autonomous technology in a neighboring market has exploded: defense. Over the past five years, VC investment in defense startups has skyrocketed from zero to $20 billion annually. His product is perfectly suited for logistical support and medical evacuation in contentious environments. But he's completely unaware of these opportunities in the defense market. Chris's team did indeed do impressive system integration (deep integration with an existing flight platform, which sets their solution apart from most competitors), and they still have business, but it's no longer the business they initially envisioned. After talking with Chris, I realized: Most startups that are more than two years old have outdated business plans, and their technology stacks and team configurations are likely obsolete. If you haven't been paying attention lately, here's what you've missed. What's changed? VC money is flowing heavily into AI. In 2025, AI projects accounted for two-thirds of total VC investment. This means that if you're not working on AI-related projects, you're competing for a smaller pool of funding. Non-AI startups must answer one question: Why can't a well-funded, native AI competitor simply eat up your market? For software founders, AI has completely rewritten the old formulas for cost, speed, and manpower. Using tools like Claude Code or OpenAI Codex for Vibe Coding, an MVP (Minimum Viable Product) can be completed in days or even hours, no longer requiring months. This also means that an MVP alone no longer proves your team's capabilities. These tools are changing the composition of development teams: there are fewer engineers, and the types of engineers are changing, with a differentiation between "business process engineers" and "deep technical engineers." What used to require a whole development team can now be done by a few people, sometimes even one. Data used to be a differentiating advantage and a moat, but current foundational models (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude) are commoditizing public data sources.

Caption: Model T vs Ferrari

The concept of Agile development itself needs to be rethought.

The bottleneck in the past was: Can we afford to build and release this product? The bottleneck now is: Do we know what to test? Can we reach users quickly enough to learn? Agile is no longer a sequential process.

AI agents can run multiple things in parallel at the same or even lower cost. You can now test multiple versions of the same business simultaneously, or even test different business directions at the same time. You can run five pricing models, ten marketing messages, and twenty UX processes simultaneously. Moreover, the "user interface" may no longer be the screen; the goal of testing may become finding a prompt that gets the AI ​​agent to deliver the expected results.

Caption: The Transformation from UI to AI Agent

The bottleneck is no longer engineering capabilities, but has shifted upwards to judgment, insight into customer expectations, and distribution capabilities.

The bottleneck is no longer engineering capabilities, but has shifted upwards to judgment, insight into customer expectations, and distribution capabilities.

AI Agent Will Rewrite All Software Categories AI Agent will change every software category, including the one you're working on. Today's software applications work like this: they show users information and then wait for them to interact with them through dashboards, alerts, workflow tools, and reports. But customers buy software to get a job done, not to look at a few more screens. Getting the job actually done is what AI Agent (orchestrated through tools like OpenClaw) will do autonomously. What does this mean? If your product currently tells users "what to do next," AI Agent will ultimately do that step for them. If your competitors' products automate tasks while yours still require a click, you're no longer competitive. Next-generation applications won't just display information on a screen; they'll act like an employee: resolving work orders, scheduling meetings, filtering leads, and automatically replenishing stock. As products shift from "software as interface" to "software as result," pricing will change from per-seat to per-result: per work order resolved, per meeting scheduled, per lead closed. (The pursuit of Product/Market Fit will shift to the pursuit of AI Agent/Customer Outcome Fit. The Minimum Viable Product (MVP) will become the Minimum Deliverable (MPO). I will elaborate on this topic in the next article.) Hardware is no exception. For hardware founders, the changes are equally dramatic. Hardware is still constrained by the laws of physics, capital, supply chains, and manufacturing cycles; you can't bypass cutting metal, creating prototypes, or tape-out. But AI allows you to eliminate bad ideas much faster. Now you can simulate more design variations, create digital twins, and stress test various hypotheses earlier and cheaper before building physical prototypes. The result is accelerated learning and discovery (and sometimes, faster failure), and in startups, faster failure is an advantage, not a disadvantage. Once AI is embedded as part of a system, the product itself changes. Adding an AI backend to a camera transforms it into a monitoring system, a vibration sensor, or a machine failure prediction system. Robots become factory workers. The moat is no longer just the hardware itself, but what the hardware can sense and what decisions and actions AI can make using that data. Companies founded before 2025 typically have technology stacks optimized for a world where software development is expensive and highly customized. Agile development and DevSecOps have made us lean, but they operate serially, and team sizes are sized accordingly. Companies that have spent years building "proprietary code and feature moats" are finding that AI is commoditizing much of their technology stack. This puts startups in an awkward position: their business models may already be partially or completely obsolete. When you're focused on product development and finding a product/market fit, these changes might not be immediately apparent. Technology stack, product features, user interface, headcount—these sunk costs can become reasons for your reluctance to transform: How can we throw away years of work? Our VCs invested in this direction. Customers still want a UI. The team believes in this roadmap. Our customers aren't ready. (Chris is a prime example. He created something truly impressive, likely still competitive, but the business model surrounding it needs to change.) Some sunk costs are actually assets: deep domain knowledge, customer relationships, proprietary data, hard-earned regulatory approvals, physical integration. These are worth preserving. Chris's flight platform integration falls into this category. The real sunk costs of debt are: large engineering teams built for slow software cycles, seat-based pricing models, and product roadmaps built around features rather than results. These are the so-called "dead moose on the table"—the problems are obvious, but nobody wants to point them out. The founders who survive are those who can look at what they've done and ask themselves: If I were to start a new business today with today's tools and in today's market, what would I actually do? This question is uncomfortable when you already have funding for a specific direction. But what's a little discomfort compared to investors telling you they're not planning to invest in the next round, and you shutting down with an outdated plan? Summary

You can't use the script from 2024 (or earlier) to run the race of 2026. Funding, technology, and business models have all changed. Agile development is becoming parallel development.

  • The pursuit of Product/Market Fit will become the pursuit of AI Agent/Customer Outcome Fit. MVP will become MPO (Minimum Deliverable).

  • A sunk cost mentality will bankrupt you.

  • A defensible moat may still exist in: proprietary data, a deep understanding of customer outcomes, regulatory lock-in, or becoming a formally acquired program (Program of Record). If you can still sleep soundly, it means you haven't figured out what's going on. Founders who survive will step out of the office, assess the situation, transform, and correct course.

data.title: The ratio changed from 1:8 to 1:1; the underrated CPU is becoming a new bottleneck for AI.
data.description:
Author: Zhao Ying, Wall Street Insights

The focus of the AI ​​computing power race is quietly shifting from GPUs to a long-neglected role—the CPU.

With the explosive growth of AI agents and reinforcement learning (RL) workloads, the strategic position of CPUs in data centers is undergoing a structural reassessment. In an in-depth interview on April 8th, Dylan Patel, chief analyst at the well-known semiconductor analysis firm SemiAnalysis, bluntly stated that the paradigm of AI workloads is evolving from simple text generation to complex agents and reinforcement learning, and CPUs are facing an "extremely severe capacity shortage."

... A recent report from market research firm TrendForce confirms this assessment: Currently, the CPU to GPU ratio in AI data centers is approximately 1:4 to 1:8, but in the era of agent-based AI, this ratio is expected to narrow significantly to 1:1 to 1:2. This structural shift has already triggered a chain reaction on both the supply and demand sides. Intel and AMD raised prices for some of their CPU product lines by the end of the first quarter of 2026. Meanwhile, Nvidia and Arm both announced their entry into the server CPU market in March 2026—a GPU giant and an IP licensor making the same choice in the same month is no coincidence, but a concentrated release of market signals. The Rise of Intelligent Agents: CPUs Become Bottlenecks In the early stages of AI development, the CPU's role was quite marginal. Dylan Patel described it as: "The workload was very light. You send a string, it sends a string back, simple inference, not much demand on the CPU." At that time, GPUs dominated AI computing power demands with their massively parallel matrix operation capabilities, and CPUs only played an auxiliary role in compressing and routing memory data to GPUs. However, the new generation of inference models, represented by OpenAI o1, and the emerging AI agent architecture, fundamentally changed this landscape. Unlike static large language models, agent AI needs to dynamically interact with the environment—planning tasks, calling tools, transferring data between sub-agents, and evaluating task completion. The entire coordination work of this "orchestration layer" falls precisely on the CPU, making it a typical CPU-intensive load. The academic paper "A CPU-Centric Perspective on Agentic AI," published in November 2025, further quantifies this pressure: in agentic AI scenarios, latency generated by CPU tool processing (including Python interpretation, web crawling, lexical summarization, database retrieval, etc.) can account for up to 90.6% of the total latency; in high-volume processing scenarios, CPU dynamic energy consumption can reach 44% of the total system dynamic energy consumption. Arm's calculations reveal the magnitude of the demand gap from a capacity perspective: traditional AI data centers require approximately 30 million CPU cores per gigawatt (GW), while in the era of agentic AI, this demand will surge to 120 million cores—a fourfold increase. Intel Under Pressure, AMD Expands Seizes Opportunity. The structural rise in CPU demand has first triggered a reshaping of the traditional x86 market. Intel's Xeon processors once held over 95% of the data center CPU market share. This dominance began to crumble in 2021—yield issues with Intel's 7-process technology delayed the release of Xeon Sapphire Rapids by nearly two years, opening a market gap for AMD's EPYC Milan. In 2026, Intel plans to launch two flagship products: the Xeon 6+ (Clearwater Forest) based on the Darkmont architecture, featuring 288 cores/288 threads and a TDP of approximately 450W; and the Xeon 7 (Diamond Rapids) based on the Panther Cove-X architecture, with up to 256 cores/256 threads and a TDP as high as 650W. Both products are based on Intel's most advanced 18A process and introduce Foveros Direct hybrid bonding technology for the first time. However, TrendForce points out that due to ongoing yield issues with the 18A process, mass production of both products may be delayed until 2027. In contrast, AMD's pace is more stable. AMD's 2026 flagship product, EPYC Venice, will utilize TSMC's N2 process and Zen 6 architecture, featuring CoWoS-L and SoIC advanced packaging. Through Simultaneous Multithreading (SMT) technology, it will achieve 256 cores/512 threads—the highest thread count currently on the market. **TrendForce predicts that AMD will continue to erode Intel's market share in 2026.** Nvidia and Arm's strong entry will rewrite the competitive landscape. Beyond the traditional x86 giants, a group of non-traditional players are flooding into the server CPU market at an unprecedented pace, fundamentally reshaping the competitive landscape. In March 2026, Nvidia announced that it would sell the Vera CPU as a standalone product to meet customers' needs for more flexible CPU:GPU configurations. Vera utilizes NVIDIA's proprietary Olympus architecture, based on TSMC's N3 process and CoWoS-R packaging, offering 88 cores/176 threads and featuring an NVLink-C2C interconnect with speeds of 1.8 TB/s, enabling memory sharing with NVIDIA GPUs. Initial partners include Alibaba, ByteDance, Cloudflare, CoreWeave, and Oracle. NVIDIA also launched the Vera CPU rack, integrating 256 CPUs per rack, totaling 22,528 cores/45,056 threads and 400 TB of total memory. In the same month, Arm announced the launch of its first self-developed CPU product, the Arm AGI CPU, ending its 35-year history as a pure licensor. Based on TSMC's N3 process and Neoverse V3 architecture, the product offers 136 cores/136 threads, a TDP of 300W, and supports DDR5-8800 memory and PCIe Gen6. Initial partners include Meta, OpenAI, Cerebras, Cloudflare, and SK Telecom. Arm simultaneously launched two rack configurations: an air-cooled version integrating 60 AGI CPUs (8,160 cores, approximately 180 TB of memory), and a liquid-cooled version supporting 336 CPUs (45,696 cores, 1 PB of memory). Major cloud service providers (CSPs) are also accelerating their deployment of self-developed CPUs. AWS will release the Graviton5 (192 cores/192 threads) based on TSMC's N3 process in December 2025, and will deploy it in conjunction with its self-developed Trainium 3 AI ASIC to reduce AI computing costs; Microsoft will launch the Cobalt 200 (N3 process, 132 cores/132 threads) in November 2025; Google plans to launch the Axion C4A.metal bare metal version and the next-generation Axion N4A in 2026, focusing on the highest cost-performance ratio. IC Back-End Design Service Providers Usher in Incremental Opportunities

The large-scale entry of non-traditional players is creating considerable incremental business for IC back-end design service providers.

TrendForce points out that AWS still insists on completing CPU back-end design in-house, while Google and Microsoft have outsourced their CPU back-end design services to Global Unichip Corp. (GUC). As more CSPs and emerging CPU manufacturers enter the market, this outsourcing demand is expected to continue to expand.

TrendForce predicts that between 2026 and 2028, ASIC design service providers such as Broadcom, Marvell, GUC, Alchip, and MediaTek will successively undertake new projects from these clients.

data.title: Dissecting RAVE's Market Manipulation Techniques
data.description:
Author: Psyduck Fomo (Psyduck); Source: X, @psyduck_fo

mo

Coin Distribution: 96% of the coins are locked in 6 multi-signature wallets, with one address holding 77%. Retail investors' holdings combined are less than 0.1%. Bitget is the main battlefield for spot trading, with 88% of exchange spot trading on Bitget.

Contract Distribution: Contract open interest has exceeded all available spot trading on exchanges, with an OI/spot ratio of 102.9%. Short sellers simply don't have enough contracts to cover their positions, a classic short squeeze deadlock.

Contract holdings: Contract open interest has exceeded all available spot trading on exchanges, with an OI/spot ratio of 102.9%. Short sellers simply don't have enough contracts to cover their positions, a classic short squeeze deadlock.

Contract holdings have exceeded all available spot trading on exchanges, with an OI/spot ratio of 102.9%.

... OKX, accounting for 64.7% of OI (On-the-Books), is the biggest powder keg. A chain reaction of liquidations on OKX would have a greater impact than all other exchanges combined. Funding rates are negative across the board, indicating the short squeeze is far from over. Regarding liquidations: $41.61 million was liquidated in 24 hours, with 73% of those liquidations being short positions. Bybit and Gate.io accounted for 53% of liquidations, making them a major disaster area for high-leverage retail investors. Gate.io's liquidation/OI ratio is a staggering 35.65x. Binance has the most effective risk control—holding 22.3% of OI but contributing only 8.3% of liquidations, with a liquidation/OI ratio of 0.37x, the lowest in the entire exchange. Tiered margin and liquidation mechanisms are indeed protecting users.

I. Distribution of Spot Tokens

Key Findings: 6 Gnosis Safe multisignature wallets control 96.36% of the supply. Among them, address #1 alone accounts for 76.94%, making it the absolute controlling address.

1.1 Team/Contract Wallet (96.41%, 11 addresses)

1.3 Personal Wallet EOA (0.06%, 54 addresses in total)

Key Findings:Retail holdings are almost negligible (total less than 600,000 RAVE). Arthur Hayes holds only 6,250 RAVE, and Amber Group market makers hold only 80,000 RAVE.

II. Contract OI (Open Interest) Distribution

Contract OI ($294 million) has exceeded the total spot market value ($294 million), with an OI/spot ratio of 102.9%. Short sellers do not have enough spot market value to cover their positions—a classic short squeeze. OKX alone accounts for 64.7% of the OI, making it the biggest potential point for a chain reaction of liquidation risks in the entire market.

2.1 OI summary of all exchanges

3.1 24-Hour Liquidation Overview

3.2 Liquidation Ranking by Exchange (24h)

3.3 Key Findings: Binance's Risk Control is Significant

Binance holds 22.3% of OI, but contributes only 8.32% of liquidations (liquidation/OI ratio: 0.37x).

...> Tiered margin, liquidation engine, and risk reserve effectively protect users during extreme market conditions. In comparison, Gate.io's liquidation/OI ratio is as high as 35.65x, while Bybit's is 2.63x.

IV. Determining the Main Battlefield

For spot trading, look at Bitget (88.3% CEX spot), for contracts, look at OKX (64.7% OI), for liquidation, look at Bybit + Gate.io (52.9% liquidation), and for risk control, look at Binance (0.37x lowest liquidation ratio).

V. List of Closing Signals for Monitoring

Current Status: The major shareholders have not yet sold off their holdings, and the short squeeze continues. However, 96% of the chips are held by 6 different shareholders, meaning the major shareholders can pump or dump the price at any time. The following are the closing signals that require close monitoring.

data.title: Citrea: Peter Thiel's Key Deployment on the Bitcoin L2 Network
data.description:
Author: Ryan Yoon, Source: Tiger Research, Translated by: Shaw Jinse Finance

Key Takeaways

  • Most Bitcoin L2 security solutions don't actually utilize Bitcoin's security mechanisms. Citrea achieves this through zero-knowledge proofs and BitVM, allowing Bitcoin to directly verify its state.

  • Founders Fund has a strong track record of discovering unique technologies (e.g., SpaceX and Palantir). Choosing Citrea when Bitcoin L2 prices were at their lowest point demonstrates their unwavering belief in the technology.

  • Founders Fund has a proven track record of discovering unique technologies (e.g., SpaceX and Palantir). Their choice of Citrea when Bitcoin L2 prices were at their lowest point also reflects their strong belief in the technology.

    • The Citrea team doesn't have any particularly illustrious credentials. However, they were the first to validate zero-knowledge proof technology in a Bitcoin production environment and launched the mainnet before most Bitcoin secondary payment systems had even gone live. The technology validation is complete. The remaining challenge is to give EVM ecosystem users a reason to use the same services on Bitcoin. The next step is to actively expand the user base. 1. L2 Skepticism and Citrea: Why Thiel Chose the latter Bitcoin's market capitalization has surpassed $2 trillion. However, when asked about the actual use of this money, the answer is unclear. "Make this money work. Utilize Bitcoin's security." This is the core logic behind the surge in Bitcoin L2 prices. But of the dozens of projects born from this logic, very few have actually been effective. The need was clear, but the results were less than satisfactory. Against this backdrop, Peter Thiel's Founders Fund invested in Citrea, choosing a team capable of building the first zero-knowledge base aggregation on Bitcoin, rather than another marketing gimmick. This was a high-conviction bet on the forefront of Bitcoin's L2 technology, made precisely at a time when the overall market was most skeptical. 2. Why Thiel chose Citrea? Founders Fund has a history of investing in unique technologies that other companies dare not venture into. Limited partners have called the investment in SpaceX "crazy." One partner at Kleiner Perkins even walked out midway through a Palantir roadshow. The investment in Citrea follows the same technological conviction. While most companies in the Bitcoin L2 space are focused on marketing over-engineered sidechains, Citrea focuses on solving the "from zero to one" problem, truly bringing zero-knowledge technology to Bitcoin. Founders Fund stepped in as market confidence in Bitcoin L2 plummeted. Partner Joey Krug believes Citrea represents "the strongest team and technical architecture in the Bitcoin L2 space." As the company's first investment in the Bitcoin ecosystem, this move is not about following trends; it's about supporting the only design that can ultimately make Bitcoin's $2 trillion capital a programmable design. 2.1. A Team That Proved Its Zero-Knowledge Capabilities Before developing based on Bitcoin, the team released a "Proof of Innocence." In 2022, the U.S. Treasury Department sanctioned Tornado Cash for money laundering. The collateral damage was immediate. Innocent users with no connection to the illegal activities were also treated the same way. The team utilizes zero-knowledge proofs, allowing users to prove their wallets are not associated with sanctioned addresses without revealing their identity. RAILGUN DAO integrates this protocol. Vitalik Buterin has also publicly cited it. None of the founders come from large tech companies or well-known protocol fields. Yet, they have successfully launched a zero-knowledge sharing product that is already in production and has received external validation. Founders Fund's assessment of them as a "best team" is not unfounded.

    2.2. Solving the Core Problem of Bitcoin L2 with Zero-Knowledge Proofs

    Citrea overcomes the core obstacle of Bitcoin L2 using zero-knowledge proof technology, leveraging Bitcoin's actual security to run services on it.

    Most Bitcoin L2 security mechanisms assign security permissions to a small group of signers. This can be imagined as a vault shared by five key holders, where any three can authorize withdrawals. This model is quick and practical, but it doesn't fully inherit Bitcoin's security because it relies on the integrity of a few people. Most Bitcoin Level 2 (L2) security layers are not significantly different in structure. Design details may vary, but the reliance on a few signers remains consistent across the domain. Citrea built a 1/N system where only one honest participant is needed. For example, when a user attempts to withdraw Bitcoin from Citrea, the process is controlled by four roles: Signer: Cryptographically locks the withdrawal conditions before any request is made. Once set, the conditions cannot be changed, preventing operators from manipulating withdrawal operations. Operator: Can only process withdrawals within the limits set by the signer. Collateral must be provided in advance, and cryptographic proof of its validity must be submitted to retrieve it. **Watchtower:** Simultaneously monitors Citrea and Bitcoin. If the operator attempts a fraudulent withdrawal, the Watchtower collects evidence and passes it to the challenger. **Challenger:** Uses the evidence provided by Watchtower to invalidate the fraudulent withdrawal on Bitcoin. Once fraud is confirmed, the operator's collateral is forfeited. The system can continue to operate if any one of these four participants remains honest. However, there is a challenge: Bitcoin's base layer is not designed for complex computations. How can cryptographic proof verification and dispute resolution mechanisms operate on top of it? ZK technology and BitVM make this possible. They can implement cryptographic proof verification and dispute resolution directly on Bitcoin. Founders Fund is optimistic about this unique technology. 3. How Citrea Actually Works The following is an application of the above architecture in practice. The system is driven by three components.

    1. ZK Rollup:Compresses transaction data and publishes it to Bitcoin

    2. BitVM:Supports optimistic Bitcoin native verification

    3. Clementine:Citrea's Bitcoin bridge based on BitVM

    3.1. ZK Proof: Thousands of transactions, one proof

    Take a notary office as an example. Notarizing thousands of contracts one by one is time-consuming and expensive. A lawyer (Citrea) only needs to review all the contracts and submit a notarized document proving that "all these contracts are valid."

    The notary office (Bitcoin) only needs to review this one document. It's quick, cheap, and the final notarization record is still kept by the notary office. This certification is a zero-knowledge proof. Citrea processes thousands of transactions off-chain, generates zero-knowledge proofs using zkEVM, and records them on the Bitcoin underlying layer. Anyone running Citrea and a Bitcoin node can verify whether Citrea's state is correct. 3.2. BitVM: No Rule Changes Required One problem remains. Bitcoin was not designed to verify complex zero-knowledge proofs. Its scripting language can only handle basic transaction conditions. Running complex calculations directly on Bitcoin is virtually impossible. Citrea solves this problem using BitVM. You can think of it as a notary office's appeal window. Under normal circumstances, zero-knowledge proofs are accepted according to their submission status. If someone questions the validity of a proof, Bitcoin intervenes and verifies it directly. The computation only runs when a dispute arises; it does not run by default. Bitcoin's consensus rules remain unchanged. 3.3. **Clementine: One Honest Actor Is Enough** In any Bitcoin secondary payment system, the bridge is the weakest link. If funds are stolen during a transfer between Bitcoin and Citrea, the entire architecture fails. Citrea's bridge, Clementine, takes a different approach. This security model requires only one honest participant out of N participants. If the operator attempts fraud, the challenger uses the chainhead proof issued by Watchtower to initiate a challenge transaction directly on the Bitcoin network, forfeiting the operator's collateral. Funds cannot be stolen unless all participants are compromised simultaneously. Standard multi-signature bridges transfer funds only when two out of three people agree, while Clementine ensures fund security as long as even one honest participant is present. The result is that attacking Citrea is tantamount to attacking Bitcoin itself. This requires controlling more than half of the total computing power to block challenges during the disputed window, which is fundamentally different from threatening a small group of signers. 4. The Current State of Citrea Most projects conduct token generation (TGE) before launching the mainnet. Testnets allow failures without consequences. This model is common: sell the vision first, then postpone the technology launch. But the mainnet is different. It involves real funds, real transactions, and real accountability. Citrea did the opposite. The mainnet launched before the token generation exchange (TGE), along with cBTC (bridged to Citrea via BTC) and ctUSD (Citrea's native stablecoin), with over 40 applications already running at launch. Applications such as Satsuma, Signals, Zentra, Crest, and CellFi all belong to the Citrea Origins project, which is directly supported by the Citrea team. Unlike most Bitcoin Level 2 (L2) cryptocurrencies that focus on staking and yield, Citrea directly supports developers in the DEX, prediction market, money market, privacy trading, and payments sectors. The intention is clear: to expand the practical functionality of Bitcoin L2 and build genuine usability. Despite this, Citrea is still in its early stages. The TVL is approximately $6 million, and while the testnet event attracted 33,000 participants, this momentum has not yet carried over to the mainnet, even after the launch of the new mainnet event. The network has been online for less than three months, and key services, including the Morpho Vault, were only recently introduced. Citrea's long-term vision is to become a hub for Bitcoin applications, building an ecosystem that goes far beyond staking and yielding. The goal is clear, but user acquisition is still underway. 5. Design vs. Reality Web3 is rife with projects peddling ideas: decentralization, censorship resistance, financial sovereignty. These ideas sound easy, but actually getting funding is difficult. Citrea's technical solution is sound. Bitcoin L2 should truly utilize Bitcoin's security mechanisms. Two trillion dollars of idle funds. Zero-knowledge proofs are the only way to achieve this. The Founders Fund agrees, and Citrea has successfully launched its mainnet. This distinguishes it from projects that only talk the talk. Next comes a more thorny issue. Most Bitcoin holders are not uneasy. Holding Bitcoin is perfectly normal. The idea of ​​"building on Bitcoin" doesn't appeal to ordinary users. Secure infrastructure is a necessary, but not sufficient, condition. What Citrea needs now is not more technical proofs, but rather to make the operations users perform on Arbitrum and Base work better on Bitcoin. This is precisely the mission of the Origins project (decentralized exchanges, money markets, privacy trading, prediction markets). If these applications cannot attract users, then the infrastructure will remain just infrastructure. The foundation is laid, the highway is built, and the traffic lights are installed. Now all that's left is to wait for the cars. No matter how well the road is paved, without traffic, it's just an empty asphalt road. The next challenge for Citrea isn't building a better road, but finding drivers.

data.title: Internal strife erupts at decentralized AI project Bittensor: Covenant AI team withdraws.
data.description: The capital market's faith in "decentralized AI" (DeAI) is facing an unprecedented stress test. Recently, Bittensor ($TAO), the undisputed leader in the decentralized AI field, suffered a devastating internal earthquake. Covenant AI, one of the top development teams in the Bittensor ecosystem, which had just successfully trained a 72-byte language model, suddenly announced its complete withdrawal from the Bittensor network via social media. In its departure statement, Covenant AI directly criticized Bittensor founder Jacob Steeves, accusing him of having "absolute and dictatorial" control over the network, arbitrarily cutting off token rewards for subnets, and stating that the so-called decentralized AI was nothing more than a carefully orchestrated "drama." Affected by this black swan event, the price of the $TAO token experienced a panic sell-off in the secondary market, with a single-day drop of 15% to 25%, instantly wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in market value. While the crypto community was observing the public break between the top team and the founder, it also began to seriously examine a deeper industry question: In the AI ​​field, which heavily relies on computing power and complex engineering, is the "decentralization" driven by token economics a utopia that reshapes production relations, or merely a glamorous cloak to conceal centralized power? To understand the destructive power of this incident, we must first recognize the significance of Covenant AI within the Bittensor ecosystem. In Bittensor's multi-subnet architecture, most subnets are still at the low-level API call, model fine-tuning, or simple task routing stages. Teams truly capable of training from scratch or large-scale parametric models are extremely rare. Covenant AI is precisely the "hardcore" representative of this ecosystem. Shortly before announcing their withdrawal, the team delivered a milestone to the community: successfully training a massive open-source model with 72 billion parameters (72B) in a decentralized network environment. At current computing power costs, training a 72B model requires mobilizing a massive GPU cluster (essentially equivalent to thousands of H100 GPUs running continuously for weeks) and incurring extremely high hardware and electricity costs. Covenant AI's willingness to bear such huge upfront sunk costs stemmed from Bittensor's "Emissions" mechanism—as long as its provided models and computing power achieved high scores in subnet evaluations, it would continuously receive $TAO token releases as substantial rewards. This is precisely the most attractive flywheel effect in the DeAI narrative. However, the flywheel came to an abrupt halt at its climax. According to Covenant AI's disclosure, after investing heavily in training and launching its 72B model, founder Jacob Steeves and his stakeholders, by controlling the validators, directly cut off the token rewards flowing to the Covenant AI subnet without any warning or transparent governance process. For miners and developers, cutting off Emissions is tantamount to "pulling the plug." The ROI of huge computing power expenditures instantly became zero. This extremely unpredictable systemic risk directly triggered Covenant AI's angry exit. The word "charade" used by Covenant AI in its exit statement precisely struck Bittensor's most vulnerable nerve: network control. Bittensor's underlying design relies on the Yuma consensus, the core of which is that "validators" evaluate the contributions of "miners" and determine how the newly issued $TAO tokens are distributed. Theoretically, this is a decentralized game system based on staking and algorithms. However, Covenant AI's accusations reveal a harsh reality: **computing power is decentralized, but power and capital are highly concentrated.** In the current Bittensor root network, the top validator nodes that dominate token distribution have their staking highly concentrated in the addresses associated with early investors, the foundation, and founder Jacob Steeves. This means the founder is not only the rule-maker but also the ultimate referee. Covenant AI points out that when a subnet's output does not align with Jacob's personal wishes or may threaten the interests of other "direct descendant" subnets, Jacob can easily use his massive staking weight to alter the Yuma consensus distribution. This "one-man show" intervention renders the decentralization at the smart contract level meaningless. Developers spend millions of dollars on computing power, yet their fate ultimately depends on the subjective will or behind-the-scenes manipulation of a single founder. Objectively speaking, Jacob and his supporters might defend this by citing reasons such as "maintaining the overall quality of the network" and "preventing specific subnets from exploiting loopholes to farm coins." However, in the absence of a transparent DAO governance mechanism, on-chain hearings, and appeal channels, this centralized intervention, acting as a "benevolent authority," severely undermines the network's core value as a "trustworthy and neutral infrastructure." The 15-25% single-day plunge in $TAO is not merely a panic sell-off by retail investors, but rather a repricing of Bittensor's "governance risk discount" by institutional investors. Bittensor's massive market capitalization and high valuation premium stem from the market's perception of it as the only realistic target for "decentralized OpenAI." The foundation of this grand narrative is that the system must possess extremely high predictability: as long as you contribute computing power and a high-quality model, the protocol will automatically guarantee your returns through code. The Covenant AI incident shattered this expectation. Top financial professionals and institutional investors abhor "unpredictable single-node failures," and in this case, the point of failure was Jacob Steeves' power. If even a top-tier team capable of training a 72B model can instantly lose everything due to the founder's intervention, then for other computing power providers and AI research institutions holding tokens and waiting to see what happens, deploying heavy assets on Bittensor is undoubtedly a Russian roulette game where the table could be overturned at any moment. When high-quality supply-side resources (miners and developers) refuse to enter due to fear of centralized tyranny, the application scenarios and intrinsic value of the $TAO token become unsustainable. The frantic exodus of funds is a preemptive vote against this deteriorating fundamental situation. The departure of Covenant AI is not merely a public relations crisis for Bittensor, but an inevitable growing pain for the entire decentralized AI field as it enters deeper waters. It brutally reveals the "impossible triangle" of DeAI: model quality and scale, decentralized trust neutrality, and incentive alignment to prevent malicious behavior. Centralization of Scale vs. Decentralization of Mechanisms: Training cutting-edge AI (such as large models exceeding 72 bytes) is a typical capital-intensive, centralized project requiring highly collaborative GPU clusters. This presents a natural physical gap with the permissionless, distributed nodes advocated by Web3. **Anti-Fraudulent Traffic vs. Trusted Neutrality:** To prevent low-quality nodes from fraudulently obtaining tokens through mutual traffic manipulation (a Sybil attack), the network must introduce subjective "quality assessment." However, with AI assessment standards not yet fully objective and mathematically standardized, entrusting this assessment power to a few validators can easily degenerate into centralized rent-seeking. Bittensor attempted to bridge this gap with token economics, but the Covenant incident proved that the foundation of this bridge (governance mechanism) remains extremely fragile. The departure of Covenant AI punctured the romantic bubble of Bittensor's "absolute decentralization." For $TAO, this may be a painful moment of demystification, but for the entire DeAI industry, it serves as a necessary wake-up call.

data.title: Bitwise: Why Geopolitical Uncertainty Is Fueling Bitcoin's Rise
data.description:
Authors: Matt Hougan, Chief Investment Officer at Bitwise & Ryan Rasmussen, Head of Research at Bitwise; Translated by Shaw & Jinse Finance

Chaos is a ladder.

Bitcoin has performed exceptionally well since the outbreak of the conflict with Iran.Since the US and Israel began airstrikes on February 28, Bitcoin has risen 12%, while the S&P 500 has fallen 1% and gold has fallen 10%.

Performance of Bitcoin, Gold, and Stocks During the Iran Conflict in 2026

Source: Bitwise Asset Management, data source: Bloomberg.

data.title: Goldman Sachs plans to launch a BTC premium ETF; Tether launches a self-custodied wallet.
data.description:
Headlines

▌Goldman Sachs Plans to Launch a "Bitcoin Premium Yield ETF"

On April 14th, according to market news, Goldman Sachs has submitted an application to launch a "Bitcoin Premium Yield ETF".

▌Tether Launches Self-Custodial Digital Wallet tether.wallet

On April 14th, according to official news, Tether announced the launch of tether.wallet, a self-custodial digital wallet that puts Tether's global financial infrastructure directly in the hands of users. With the launch of tether.wallet, this funds distribution network is directly open to end users for the first time.

... As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $74,191.84, a 24-hour change of -0.6%; ETH price is $2,325.68, a 24-hour change of -2.1%; BNB price is $614.25, a 24-hour change of -0.3%. SOL price is $83.95, a 24-hour change of -2.9%; DOGE price is $0.09308, a 24-hour change of -1.2%; XRP price is $1.36, a 24-hour change of -1.0%; TRX price is $0.3237, a 24-hour change of +0.8%. The price of WLFI is $0.08066, with a 24-hour change of -1.6%; the price of HYPE is $43.47, with a 24-hour change of -1.9%. Policy: ▌US Senators plan to release draft amendments to stablecoin yields this week, potentially impacting market structure legislation. On April 14th, US Senator Thom Tillis stated that the Senate is expected to release the text of a draft amendment regarding stablecoin yield distribution this week. Currently, banks and crypto companies disagree on whether to allow cryptocurrency exchanges to pay stablecoin yields to stablecoin holders through reward programs, a dispute that has stalled the Clarity Act legislation process. A recent report from the White House Council of Economic Advisers stated that banning stablecoin yields would have a minimal impact on small banks, increasing lending by only 0.02%. However, the American Banking Association believes this analysis underestimates the risks. Observers point out that if the draft regulations are too strict, it could lead to a flow of users and liquidity to other jurisdictions that allow yields.

▌People's Daily criticizes "100x coins" and other practices that use false profit information to attract traffic

On April 14, People's Daily published an article titled "Disrupting the Capital Market: These Irregularities in Financial Self-Media Accounts Need to Be Wary Of," specifically naming some accounts that use false profit information such as "100x coins" and "making millions a month trading cryptocurrencies" to attract traffic to virtual currency trading.

The article again cites a previous notice from eight departments including the People's Bank of China, emphasizing that virtual currency-related business activities are illegal financial activities, and points out that accounts and platforms providing traffic and technical services for illegal virtual currency transactions are suspected of violating laws and regulations, and may threaten financial security and anti-money laundering efforts.

▌Foreign Ministry: If the US imposes tariffs on China under the pretext of Iran, China will resolutely retaliate

On April 14, Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun hosted a regular press conference. A reporter asked, "Recently, some media reported that China is providing military support to Iran, and US President Trump stated that if it is found that China is providing weapons to Iran, the US will impose a 50% tariff on Chinese goods. What is China's comment on this?" Guo Jiakun said, "We have already stated China's position. We would like to reiterate that China has always adopted a prudent and responsible attitude towards military exports, and implements strict controls in accordance with its own export control laws and regulations and its international obligations. The relevant reports are pure fabrication.

... "If the US insists on using this as a pretext to impose additional tariffs on China, China will resolutely retaliate," said Guo Jiakun. (Beijing Daily) The US State Department issued a statement on April 15 following a trilateral meeting between Israel, Lebanon, and the US in Washington. The statement noted that the meeting was chaired by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. The participants had a "productive" discussion on various measures to promote the initiation of direct negotiations between Israel and Lebanon. The US statement emphasized that any ceasefire agreement must be reached by both governments under US mediation. The US also pointed out that the negotiations are expected to promote Lebanon's reconstruction and economic recovery, and expand regional investment opportunities. Israel expressed its support for dismantling all non-state armed forces in Lebanon and removing related "terrorist infrastructure," and pledged to resolve differences through direct negotiations to achieve lasting peace and regional stability. Lebanon reiterated its commitment to the full implementation of the November 2024 ceasefire declaration, emphasizing national sovereignty and territorial integrity, and calling for concrete measures to alleviate the severe humanitarian crisis caused by the ongoing conflict. All parties agreed to formally launch direct negotiations at a time and place agreed upon by Israel and Lebanon. (CCTV News)

Blockchain Applications

▌Ethereum Foundation Launches Ethereum Audit Subsidy Program

On April 14, the Ethereum Foundation launched the Ethereum Audit Subsidy Program, a collaborative project with audit service providers aimed at subsidizing the cost of security audits for Ethereum developers. Security audits are industry best practices, but are expensive. The goal of this subsidy program is to make audits more affordable, thereby enhancing the security of the entire Ethereum ecosystem.

▌NVIDIA Releases Ising: World's First Open-Source AI Model to Accelerate Practical Quantum Computer Progress

On April 14th, NVIDIA released its Ising open-source model series, providing world-leading artificial intelligence (AI)-based quantum processor calibration capabilities. Its quantum error correction decoding speed is up to 2.5 times faster and 3 times more accurate than traditional methods.

... Leading quantum companies, academic institutions, and research labs using the Ising model include: Academia Sinica, Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory, Harvard University's John Paulson School of Engineering and Applied Sciences, Infleqtion, IQM Quantum Computers, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Advanced Quantum Testing Platform, and the UK's National Physical Laboratory (NPL).

▌CoW Swap front-end issues, users advised to temporarily refrain from using it

On April 14th, CoW DAO posted on the X platform that the CoW Swap front-end was experiencing issues and advised users to temporarily refrain from using it.

▌Kalshi teases new product launch on April 27th

On April 14th, the prediction platform Kalshi released a video teasing a new product launch scheduled for April 27th.

The video shows a green spiral forming a perpetual ring, eventually displaying the word "Timeless." The community speculates that this update may introduce a perpetual prediction market with no expiration date, breaking through the time limit of Kalshi's original event contracts and improving trading flexibility. According to market news, AI cloud platform FluidStack plans to raise approximately $1 billion at a valuation of $18 billion. Situational Awareness, an agency under Leopold Aschenbrenner, is in talks to participate and co-lead the round. This funding is expected to accelerate FluidStack's expansion in AI computing and cloud infrastructure, highlighting the trend of continued capital investment in the computing power sector. It is understood that FluidStack has previously signed cooperation agreements with several Bitcoin mining companies, including TeraWulf, Cipher Mining, and Hut 8. Cryptocurrency

According to Reuters, Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Warsh disclosed in a newly filed financial disclosure that he holds various future-oriented assets, including SpaceX and the prediction platform Polymarket. Warsh's main assets exceed $100 million, including two investments of over $50 million related to the Duquesne Family Office (managed by investor Stanley Druckenmiller). In the cryptocurrency and fintech sector, his portfolio also includes several related companies: Tenderly, an Ethereum developer platform; Stashfin, described as a "new bank for consumer loans"; and LemonCash, a platform providing crypto financial services.

Michael Saylor: Perpetual Preferred Stock STRC Achieves $278 Million in Daily Liquidity Michael Saylor, founder of Bitcoin treasury company Strategy, stated in an article on the X platform that his company's perpetual preferred stock STRC currently has a volatility of approximately 1.7%, a Sharpe ratio of 4.49, and daily liquidity of approximately $278 million, with overall performance approaching the stability level of money market products. He pointed out that STRC's volatility is already within the money market range, while other asset classes have not yet reached similar levels, and on this basis, STRC provides market-leading risk-adjusted returns. Paxos Labs Spins Off from Stablecoin Issuer Paxos and Raises $12 Million. Paxos Labs has spun off from stablecoin issuer Paxos and raised $12 million in funding. The round was led by Blockchain Capital, with participation from Robot Ventures, family office Maelstrom, and DeFi developer Uniswap Labs. Paxos Labs co-founder Bhaumik Kotecha declined to disclose the valuation of the round. Paxos CEO Chad Cascarilla is also the CEO of Paxos Labs. Paxos Labs will primarily provide services to large enterprises, enabling them to create and manage their own stablecoins.

▌Upbit Adds Zama (ZAMA) Listing

On April 14th, according to official news, Upbit added Zama (ZAMA) listing, supporting KRW, USDT, and BTC trading pairs.

Important Economic Developments

▌Bessant: Tariff Levels May Restore by Early July

On April 15th, US Treasury Secretary Bessant stated that Trump's tariff levels may restore to levels before the Supreme Court overturned several imposition orders by early July. After the Supreme Court ruled that Trump's use of emergency powers to impose early tariffs was unconstitutional, Bessant is seeking to rebuild his "tariff wall" using different authorizations (such as Section 301 investigations).

Bessant noted that while it's difficult to say when the consequences of the war in Iran will "impact" the US economy, the current economic situation remains strong. He believes that the US growth rate this year could easily exceed 3% or even 3.5%. He added that the continued decline in core inflation, excluding volatile energy and food prices, is a good sign. Bessant stated, "I think the Fed has consistently misjudged inflation; core inflation is declining. If they want to wait for clearer data before taking action, I understand, but that would mean interest rates should be lowered further." (Jinshi)

▌Bessant: The Fed's wait-and-see approach to the development of the Iranian conflict is wise

April 14th, according to US financial media Semafor, US Treasury Secretary Bessant stated at the "Semafor World Economy" conference in Washington, D.C. on Monday that the Federal Reserve should "wait and see" before deciding whether to lower interest rates during the war in Iran.

Regarding whether interest rates should be lowered, Bessant said, "They will eventually be lowered. But I think we have to wait and see how the economy develops. But I think that going into January, and then into January and February—the economy performed very strongly." Bessant stated that the Fed's current approach of observing the development of the conflict in Iran is "wise." Bessant said, "If [the ECB] raises [interest rates], I would be shocked. But I would say that many European countries (like the UK) and Asian countries are subsidizing demand, while we in the US are not doing so." Bessant stated that he believes the recent price increases will not be "included in inflation expectations," and he believes that the current inflation is temporary. (Jinshi) A source familiar with internal discussions in Tehran revealed that Iran is considering temporarily suspending shipping through the Strait of Hormuz to avoid challenging the US maritime blockade and undermining a new round of peace talks. The source indicated that this potential suspension reflects Iran's desire to avoid an immediate escalation of the situation at a sensitive diplomatic moment as it prepares for logistical arrangements for the next face-to-face meeting with Washington and Tehran. The source stated that suspending maritime activity for several days is seen as a feasible and pragmatic measure to prevent events that could derail fragile efforts to resume negotiations. They added that Iran's considerations are still evolving. For example, the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps might quickly change tactics, attempting to demonstrate that the US blockade can be challenged without consequences, a move that could potentially disrupt the diplomatic track.

▌Senate Banking Committee Chairman: Warsh's Nomination Expected to be Confirmed

On April 14, according to Fox Business Network, Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Warsh has cleared the hurdles preventing his confirmation. Senator Tim Scott, Chairman of the Senate Banking Committee, stated that Warsh's hearing will be held next week. He expects Warsh's nomination to be confirmed. He is confident that the Justice Department will soon conclude its investigation into Powell.

▌Trump Says Talks with Iran "Possibly Within the Next Two Days" in Pakistan

On April 14, US President Trump told the New York Post that talks with Iran "possibly within the next two days" in Pakistan.

(Xinhua) On April 15, local time, US Vice President Vance expressed optimism about the current developments in the US-Iran situation. He also noted that significant distrust exists between the US and Iran, which cannot be resolved overnight. It was learned on the 14th that sources in the US indicated Vance is expected to lead a possible second round of US-Iran talks. Sources said that Trump's special envoy Witkov and his son-in-law Kushner will also participate. Trump has assigned the task of promoting a diplomatic solution to these three individuals and continues to maintain contact with Iran and intermediaries. Currently, the US is internally discussing arrangements for the second round of talks, but has not yet determined whether it will be held or the specific time. (CCTV) ▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in April is 98.4% ... Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits an asymmetry: it falls with stocks but doesn't rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an overabundance of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," capable of functioning when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: Golden Web3.0 Daily Report | Kraken Receives $200 Million Investment from Deutsche Börse
data.description:
DeFi data

1. Total market value of DeFi tokens: US$53.928 billion

DeFi Total Market Cap Data Source: coingecko

2. Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours: $50.08

Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours. Data source: coingecko

3. Assets locked in DeFi: $985.52 billion$100 million

Top 10 DeFi Projects by Locked Assets and Total Value Locked. Data Source: defillama

NFT Data

1. Total Market Value of NFTs: $7.564 billion

2.24 hours NFT transaction volume 1.673 billion US dollars

Headlines

Kraken Receives $200 Million Investment from Deutsche Börse

Kraken's parent company, Payward, has received a $200 million investment from Deutsche Börse Group. This transaction will give Deutsche Börse a 1.5% fully diluted stake in Kraken, valuing Kraken at approximately $13.3 billion, down from $20 billion last November. The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter, but is still subject to regulatory approval.

    AI Hot Topics

    1. American Express Launches AI Payment Development Tool and Will Explore Stablecoin Settlement Application Scenarios

    Payment giant American Express announced an accelerated deployment in the AI ​​payment field and launched a developer toolkit for "Agentic Commerce," promising to provide purchase protection for erroneous transactions generated by registered AI agents. This toolkit aims to support AI agents in completing economic activities such as payments on behalf of users, while reducing fraud and dispute risks through mechanisms such as only granting payment credentials to verified agents and verifying cardholder identity. Furthermore, American Express pointed out that it already has a mature mechanism for handling transaction disputes, which can provide protection against AI agent malfunctions, thereby increasing user trust and driving transaction volume growth. It has also developed "multiple plans" for stablecoins, which are reportedly exploring stablecoin settlement scenarios rather than payments, but specific details were not disclosed.

    2. Institution: Q1 Doubao Overseas Version Downloads Exceed 72 Million, Up 47% Quarter-on-Quarter

    April 14th - According to the latest data from Sensor Tower, the downloads of native generative AI applications exceeded 1.1 billion in Q1 2026, with in-app purchase revenue increasing by 14% quarter-on-quarter to $1.9 billion. Among them, AI smart assistant applications contributed 78% of the downloads and 85% of the in-app purchase revenue. ByteDance's AI smart assistant application Dola (the overseas version of Doubao) exceeded 72 million downloads this quarter, a 47% increase quarter-on-quarter, and its cumulative downloads officially exceeded 200 million this quarter, with an average DAU of nearly 12 million in March.

    ...

    3. Venus Protocol Announces the Cessation of Isolated Pools, Withdrawals Remain Open

    Venus Protocol announced on the X platform that isolated pools on Venus have ceased operation. All pools are suspended, but user funds are safe, and withdrawals remain open. Users can choose to migrate or withdraw their assets depending on whether they are on Venus Core.

    4. Seedance 2.0 Fully Opens API Services

    On April 14th, Volcano Engine officially launched the Seedance 2.0 series of API services. Enterprise and individual users can now utilize its video generation capabilities to reshape workflows and explore new product forms and application scenarios. Simultaneously, Volcano Engine provides a series of supporting capabilities for enterprise and individual users, centered around secure creation.

    ... For example, users can easily complete facial verification and portrait authorization through the Volcano Ark console. After authorization, the image can be used for creative purposes, balancing efficiency and compliance.

    DeFi Hot Topics

    1. Tether Launches Self-Custed Digital Wallet tether.wallet

    On April 14th, according to official news, Tether announced the launch of tether.wallet, a self-custodied digital wallet that puts Tether's global financial infrastructure directly in the hands of users. With the launch of tether.wallet, this funds distribution network is directly opened to end users for the first time.

    2. Rakuten Wallet Officially Launches XRP On April 14th, Rakuten Wallet, a cryptocurrency exchange under the Rakuten Group, officially announced the listing of XRP as a tradable asset and payment method. Users can purchase XRP using loyalty points and spend it throughout Japan. 3. Upbit Adds Zama (ZAMA) On April 14th, according to official news, Upbit added Zama (ZAMA) to its trading platform, supporting KRW, USDT, and BTC trading pairs. 4. Ethereum Chain USDT and USDC Activity Has Dropped to Lowest Levels in 2026 According to data from Santiment, on April 14th, Ethereum chain USDT and USDC activity has dropped to their lowest levels since 2026, indicating declining purchasing power. 5. Aptos Releases Token Economics Update, Staking Reward Rate Reduced to 2.6% and Gas Fees Increased On April 14th, Aptos released a token economics update. Key adjustments include: the annualized staking reward rate has been reduced from 5.19% to 2.6%; gas fees have increased tenfold (while stablecoin transfer costs remain as low as approximately $0.00014); the launch of DecibelDEX will significantly increase on-chain transaction volume and gas fee burning, with an estimated burning of over 32 million APT annually; a hard cap has been set at 2.1 billion APT for the protocol layer; the Aptos Foundation will permanently lock and stake 210 million APT; future incentives will be converted to milestone triggers; and programmatic buyback programs will be explored. Disclaimer: Jinse Finance, as a blockchain information platform, provides articles for informational purposes only and does not constitute actual investment advice. Please establish sound investment principles and be sure to enhance your risk awareness.

data.title: Will the number of "deceptive flings" occur again this time, once it breaks through 75,000?
data.description:
Over the past two weeks, despite the continued sideways movement of Bitcoin prices, the open interest in CME Bitcoin futures has fallen from a daily average of $8.41 billion in March to $7.2 billion in early April, a 14-month low; daily trading volume has even fallen below $3 billion, almost returning to the levels of early last year. The main reason for the continued shrinkage of open interest is the large-scale liquidation of "basis trading," which once dominated the CME market. As Bitcoin prices have fallen from above $120,000 to around $70,000, the annualized basis yield has compressed significantly from 15%–20% a year ago to about 5% today, essentially approaching the risk-free interest rate of about 4.5%. After deducting financing costs, transaction fees, and counterparty risk, the actual arbitrage opportunity has almost disappeared, prompting leveraged funds to take profits and withdraw from the market. In other words, with noise traders leaving the market en masse, the supply-demand imbalance premium previously driven by speculative funds is gradually fading, making it increasingly difficult for short-term arbitrageurs to capitalize on mispricing opportunities, which is why the market is in a state of flux. However, despite the large-scale exit of retail investors, institutional funds continue to accumulate. According to data disclosed by MSTR, since March, the company has significantly increased its Bitcoin purchases, with this week's buying volume already recovering to the same level as last year, and showing a further upward trend.

At the same time, CryptoQuant's statistics on Ethereum holding address balance changes also show that retail investors are exiting the market while institutions are accelerating their hoarding.

... As shown in the figure, the behavior of ETH addresses with different holding sizes is significantly differentiated: small holders with 100-1,000 ETH have seen a long-term decline in their balances, with small and medium-sized retail investors continuously distributing their holdings; medium-sized holders with 1,000-10,000 ETH have accelerated their selling since October 11th of last year, and their balances have fallen to a near five-year low; while holders with 10,000-100,000 ETH... The balances of medium-to-large ETH whales increased significantly between 2025 and 2026 and are still accumulating recently, with an additional 230,000 ETH added in the past week. Furthermore, super whales with over 100,000 ETH have relatively low overall holdings, but since October 11th of last year, they have shifted from distribution to accumulation, and their balances have increased for seven consecutive months. The starkly different operating styles of large and small retail investors indicate that the market is in a structural transition period of "retail investor exit and institutional takeover." In the short term, there is a lack of catalysts for an upward breakout, but the downside is also limited by the continued accumulation by institutions. In the medium to long term, the institutional trend will drive the market from "capital speculation" to "value support," gradually moving towards maturity and stability. Although time is favorable for both sides, past experience tells us that retail investors are most sensitive to time costs and seek short-term gains; while institutions are the opposite, willing to wait 1-2 years or even longer for a new bull market. Against this backdrop, investors should focus more on the fundamentals and long-term value of their investments and avoid frequent short-term trading. As investors gradually become less sensitive to geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East, the crypto market is showing signs of capital inflows and improved risk appetite. In the past seven days, RAVE has surged thousands of times, becoming the most eye-catching phenomenon in the market. This extreme performance is not an isolated event, but rather a microcosm of increased marginal market activity. After leveraged funds are gradually cleared out, existing funds are beginning to concentrate on high-elasticity, low-market-cap stocks, attempting to find new sources of excess returns beyond the mainstream coins dominated by institutions. At the same time, the risk appetite of some speculative funds has shifted from "risk aversion and observation" to "tentative participation," and the RAVE surge has become a concentrated release of extremely suppressed speculative sentiment. While RAVE's surge may be short-lived, it has acted as a leading indicator, signaling a positive trend that altcoins, whose valuations have been severely compressed, are stabilizing and rebounding. Historically, each altcoin rally typically begins with extreme profitability in a few individual coins, gradually spreading to other coins within the sector. Although RAVE's surge is unsustainable, it has successfully attracted market attention to undervalued, highly volatile altcoins, providing a sentiment anchor for subsequent tentative investment. If the trend of improved liquidity and rising risk appetite continues, the altcoin market is expected to move from a single dominant player to a more diversified landscape. Currently, the Middle East crisis remains the biggest uncertainty affecting global capital markets. However, the market has largely priced in the worst-case scenario and tail risks, and a reversal in sentiment is highly likely once the situation shows signs of easing. From a geopolitical perspective, Iran has expressed its willingness to continue negotiations and abide by the ceasefire agreement; meanwhile, the Trump administration in the United States faces pressure from the midterm elections and urgently needs to balance high oil prices with voter war-weariness—its approval rating has fallen to its lowest point since returning to the White House, with only 36% of the public approving of its performance. Time is becoming Trump's biggest enemy. Both winning voter support and stabilizing energy prices are driving forces to de-escalate the situation. If subsequent negotiations make substantial progress and navigation in the Strait of Hormuz returns to normal, a drop in oil prices will directly alleviate inflationary pressures, creating space for the Federal Reserve to shift its monetary policy, thus significantly boosting risk assets such as cryptocurrencies. Operationally, the trend of time favoring the bulls is clear, but the path to an upward trend remains complex. Even if Bitcoin breaks through $75,000, the possibility of a rapid upward surge is still low, and a pullback is still possible after the breakthrough. For long-term investors, as long as they remain patient, ignore short-term fluctuations and noise, and focus on the gradual consolidation of value support under institutional trends, they can accumulate positions during the fluctuations and wait for the next major upward wave driven by improved liquidity and fundamentals. In short, short-term anxiety and divergence are precisely the best window for long-term positioning.
